latest.content:

1 July 2026
GSTechnologies Limited
("GST" or the "Company")
US$5 million Unsecured Loan Facility Drawdown
GSTechnologies Limited (LSE: GST), the fintech company, announces, further to the Company's announcement on 17 June 2026, that it has drawn down the first US$5 million tranche of the US$10 million unsecured term loan facility entered into with Clarivan Group Kommanditbolag (the "Facility").
The second tranche of the Facility will be available to draw down between 1 August and 30 August 2026 at the Company's election.

latest.title: US$10 million Unsecured Loan Facility
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 5565I
GSTechnologies Ltd
17 June 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF REGULATION (EU) 596/2014 AS IT FORMS PART OF DOMESTIC LAW IN THE UNITED KINGDOM BY VIRTUE OF THE EU (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018.
17 June 2026
GSTechnologies Limited
("GST", "GSTechnologies" or the "Company")
US$10 million Unsecured Loan Facility
GSTechnologies Limited (LSE: GST), the fintech company, is pleased to announce that it has entered into a US$10 million unsecured term loan facility agreement with Clarivan Group Kommanditbolag, a company incorporated in Sweden (the "Lender") (the "Facility").
The Company intends to utilise the Facility to provide additional working capital and financial flexibility as it progresses its strategic growth initiatives, including for potential strategic acquisitions.
Tone Goh, Chairman of GSTechnologies, commented:
"The Facility provides GST with access to significant additional capital on attractive terms, enhancing our financial flexibility as we continue to execute our growth strategy. In particular, the Facility strengthens our ability to pursue strategic acquisition opportunities while supporting the ongoing development of our existing businesses and initiatives."
Key Terms of the Facility
·
Unsecured term loan facility of up to US$10 million.
·
The Facility is available in two tranches: US$5 million from 1 July to 31 July 2026; and US$5 million from 1 August to 30 August 2026.
·
Interest accrues on amounts drawn under the Facility at a rate of 5 per cent. per annum and is payable monthly in arrears.
·
The proceeds of the Facility may be used for general working capital purposes and potential corporate opportunities, including strategic acquisitions.
·
The Facility has a maturity date of 31 July 2030.
·
The Facility is repayable in full on 31 July 2030 unless prepaid, redeemed or converted in accordance with its terms.
·
Subject to the terms of the Facility agreement, the Lender may elect to receive repayment of the outstanding principal amount of the Facility together with accrued interest through the issue of a new class of non-voting preference shares to be created by the Company, with such shares carrying preferential dividend and liquidation rights.
·
Any conversion into preference shares would be based on the average ten-day volume weighted average price of the Company's ordinary shares immediately preceding the date of the conversion notice and would remain subject to applicable legal and regulatory requirements and any necessary shareholder approvals.
·
The Company may elect to prepay all or part of the Facility together with accrued interest in cash, ordinary shares and/or preference shares in the Company, subject to the terms of the Facility agreement.
·
The Company may elect to redeem the Facility in full prior to maturity, subject to payment of a redemption fee equal to 1 per cent. of the outstanding principal amount redeemed.
·
The Facility agreement contains customary representations, warranties, undertakings and events of default for an agreement of this nature, including restrictions on the incurrence of additional indebtedness.
Enquiries:

latest.title: Semnet Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 3336G
GSTechnologies Ltd
01 June 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF REGULATION (EU) 596/2014 AS IT FORMS PART OF DOMESTIC LAW IN THE UNITED KINGDOM BY VIRTUE OF THE EU (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018.
1 June 2026
GSTechnologies Limited
("GST" or the "Company")
Semnet Update
GSTechnologies Limited (LSE: GST), the fintech company, provides an update to the announcement of 13 February 2026 regarding the Company's 66.67% owned subsidiary Semnet Pte. Ltd ("Semnet").
On 26 May 2026, hearings were held before the Assistant Registrar in the Singapore Supreme Court.
At these hearings, applications for a stay of proceedings numbered SUM 1210 (filed by the Semnet sellers) and SUM 1211 (filed by Chong Hoi Yan- a former Semnet employee), were dismissed in their entirety. A costs order of S$18,000 was awarded to Semnet.
A deadline to file a notice of appeal against the above decisions is set for 9 June 2026. Furthermore, a hearing will be held on 4 June 2026 for the Court to give the defence a direction for their case.
The GST Board remains committed to protecting the interests of GST shareholders and will announce further updates as appropriate.

latest.title: Temporary Suspension Of Crypto Trading Services
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 1673Y
GSTechnologies Ltd
26 March 2026
26 March 2026
GSTechnologies Limited
("GST" or the "Company")
Temporary Suspension Of Crypto Asset Trading Services
GSTechnologies Ltd (LSE: GST), the fintech company, announces the temporary suspension of the Company's crypto asset trading services with effect from 15 April 2026.
On 31 December 2025 the Company announced that GS Fintech UAB's crypto asset trading platform had transitioned to operate under the VASP-registered entity Finferno Spółka Z Ograniczoną Odpowiedzialnością ("Finferno") in Poland.
Whilst this can continue until 30 June 2026 the Company has determined that the Company's crypto asset trading activities should be aligned with the requirements of the EU Regulation on Markets in Crypto-Assets ("MiCA") as soon as possible.
Accordingly, with effect from 15 April 2026, Finferno will temporarily suspend all services relating to the trading of crypto assets. The suspension will remain in place until a MiCA licence is obtained. Finferno's Terms of Service will be updated in connection with the suspension and will take effect on the same date.
Users of the platform are being informed and further announcements will be made in due course, as appropriate.

latest.title: Semnet Update
latest.content:
13 Feb 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 8371S
GSTechnologies Ltd
13 February 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF REGULATION (EU) 596/2014 AS IT FORMS PART OF DOMESTIC LAW IN THE UNITED KINGDOM BY VIRTUE OF THE EU (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018.
13 February 2026
GSTechnologies Limited
("GST" or the "Company")
Semnet Update
GSTechnologies Ltd (LSE: GST), the fintech company, announces an update regarding the Company's 66.67% owned subsidiary Semnet Pte. Ltd ("Semnet").
As announced on 18 July 2025, the Company issued a notice of arbitration to Choo Seet EE and Zheng Kang Wen Mervyn (the "Sellers"), the sellers of Semnet, as set out in the Sale and Purchase Agreement ("SPA") dated 5 December 2023. As referenced in the Company's interim results announced on 18 December 2025, a mediation hearing was held in early December 2025 with a view to agreeing a formal settlement agreement between the Sellers and the Company. However, formal settlement terms have not been reached, and consequently, Semnet has now formally issued and served a writ of summons against the Sellers and Hoi Yan ('Daphane') Chong (a former Semnet manager) to vigorously pursue claims for alleged breaches of fiduciary and contractual duties, including breaches of the SPA, that have negatively impacted the Semnet business. The total damages sought are approximately US$4.2 million.
The GST Board remains committed to protecting the interests of shareholders and will announce further updates as appropriate.

company_30s.what_they_do: GST Technologies Ltd. builds and runs digital‑asset platforms that let customers buy, sell and store cryptocurrencies.  They also offer APIs for institutional traders and provide custody solutions for firms that need regulated storage of crypto assets.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The firm sits at the heart of the growing digital‑asset ecosystem in the UK and Europe.  Its custody and trading services fill a niche that many banks and fintechs are still trying to master, and any improvement in regulatory clarity can unlock a large, fast‑growing market.
company_30s.current_state: GST has been juggling a series of regulatory and operational hiccups.  Several suspensions and audit changes have slowed growth, but the company has secured a fresh unsecured loan and completed a strategic acquisition of Metapay.  The stock is currently trading well below the levels seen before the last series of stoppages.

rns_narrative: GST has been working to stabilize its operations after a string of regulatory setbacks.  The company secured a £10 million unsecured loan to shore up liquidity and has finished acquiring Metapay, a move aimed at expanding its product suite.  Meanwhile, it temporarily suspended crypto‑trading services while it resolves compliance issues, and it has faced delays in publishing accounts and a brief arbitration notice over its Singapore subsidiary.  These events paint a picture of a firm that is still trying to keep its core business afloat while navigating a turbulent regulatory landscape.

Three main themes emerge: 1) **Financial and operational recovery** – the loan and acquisition signal a push to regain cash flow and broaden offerings; 2) **Regulatory turbulence** – repeated suspensions, audit changes, and arbitration indicate ongoing compliance challenges; 3) **Crypto‑asset focus** – Bitcoin holdings, a new treasury policy, and the temporary halt of crypto trading all point to a company still very much in the crypto business.
While the company is still in a survival mode, the recent financing and acquisition suggest it is positioning itself for a comeback once regulatory clarity improves.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet‑but‑watchful crypto crowd – people are waiting for the next regulatory green light before jumping in.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter centers on the acquisition of Metapay and the fresh loan, arguing that these moves give GST the capital and product depth to rebound.  Some posts praise the company’s resilience and see the current lows as a buying opportunity.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears focus on the repeated suspensions, audit changes and arbitration.  They argue that GST’s business is still too exposed to regulatory risk and that the company may not be able to resume trading services in a timely way.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Earlier this week the tone was cautiously optimistic, but the latest post about a temporary halt of crypto trading services has shifted sentiment toward wariness.  The community remains silent on concrete catalysts, keeping the mood low‑key.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no recent search interest, which means the market is largely unaware of GST’s current situation – a perfect backdrop for a surprise bounce if a catalyst materialises.

near_term_catalysts: A regulatory decision that lifts the temporary suspension of crypto trading services, allowing GST to resume its core revenue stream.
A positive earnings report for the quarter that shows improved cash flow or a clear path to profitability.
Announcement of a new partnership or product that leverages the Metapay acquisition, adding fresh revenue potential.
A spike in trading volume that exceeds the recent average by at least 30 % – a classic sign that the market is beginning to re‑engage.
Any official confirmation that the company’s UK listing will remain active and that there are no imminent delisting notices.
expect_next.watch_for: Track the following signals over the next 2‑3 months: 1) Official regulatory clearance or a press release stating the suspension is lifted; 2) Q2 earnings that show a turnaround in cash burn; 3) Volume data that breaks out of the 20‑day moving average by a significant margin; 4) Any new product launch or partnership announcement.
expect_next.timing_regime: GST appears to be in a late‑stage washout – the company has already gone through a series of setbacks.  The APEX score suggests some compression, but the stock is still far from its pre‑suspension highs.  A mean‑reversion bounce would likely start once the regulatory green light arrives, so the next 3‑6 months are the critical window.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Speculative Long – the stock is still very risky, but a clear regulatory lift could trigger a sizeable rebound.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the temporary suspension is lifted and GST resumes trading, the company could quickly recover the valuation it had before the stoppages.  The recent loan and acquisition give it a stronger balance sheet, and the market’s lack of attention means the upside potential is high relative to the current low price.  A bounce to even half of its pre‑suspension level would deliver a large return on a small investment.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Regulatory uncertainty remains the biggest threat.  If the UK regulator decides to impose stricter rules or if the company fails to meet the conditions for lifting the suspension, GST could face further declines or even delisting.  A continued downturn would leave investors with a deeply discounted, but potentially worthless, stake.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: The downside is capped by the current price – the worst you could lose is the amount you put in if the stock falls to zero.  The upside is potentially several times that amount if the company clears its regulatory hurdles and re‑engages its customer base.  The risk/reward ratio is therefore highly favourable for a contrarian long stance, provided you stay disciplined with stops.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter once the suspension is officially lifted or a clear earnings beat is announced.  Use a 5‑10 % position sizing rule.  Place a stop just below the most recent swing low to protect against a temporary reversal.  Aim to take profit at the first major support level you see on the chart.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a volume spike that breaks the 20‑day average by at least 30 % or a news headline announcing a new product or partnership.  These are strong confirmations that the market is starting to move in the right direction.
playbook.if_passing: If the company is delisted from the exchange, or if it files for bankruptcy, stop trading immediately and cut any exposure.  These terminal events would wipe out any upside expectation.

ai_insights.bottom_line: GST is a deeply troubled crypto platform that could bounce dramatically once regulatory hurdles are cleared, but the risk of continued decline remains high.
